ddls:
CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "organisation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the organization where the individual is registered */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the individual */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the individual */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the individual */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the individual */,
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the family the individual belongs to */,
  "is_head_of_family" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the individual is the head of the family */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the individual was registered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the individual (e.g., OPEN, CLOSED) */,
  "label" TEXT /* Unique label for the individual or family unit */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the individual */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the individual */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "address" TEXT /* Current address of the individual */,
  "medical_conditions" TEXT /* Any medical conditions the individual has */,
  "allergies" TEXT /* Any allergies the individual has */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language spoken by the individual */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_organisation_id FOREIGN KEY ("organisation_id") REFERENCES organisations ("organisation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("family_id") REFERENCES families ("family_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "organisations" (
  "organisation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each organization */,
  "organisation_name" TEXT /* Name of the organization */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the organization */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("organisation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family unit */,
  "head_of_family_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual who is the head of the family */,
  "family_size" INTEGER /* Number of members in the family */,
  "family_status" TEXT /* Current status of the family (e.g., OPEN, CLOSED) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the family unit */,
  "primary_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact for the family */,
  "primary_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the primary contact */,
  "income_source" TEXT /* Source of income for the family */,
  "income_amount" TEXT /* Monthly or annual income amount */,
  "housing_status" TEXT /* Current housing status (e.g., rented, owned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_families_head_of_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("head_of_family_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "service_description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of service (e.g., Food, Shelter, Medical) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the service (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the service (e.g., 1 hour, 1 day) */,
  "cost" TEXT /* Cost of the service */,
  "eligibility_criteria" TEXT /* Eligibility criteria for the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_provision" (
  "provision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service provision event */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual receiving the service */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service provided */,
  "provision_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was provided */,
  "provision_status" TEXT /* Status of the service provision (e.g., Completed, Pending) */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the service provided */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the service provision */,
  "follow_up_date" TEXT /* Date for follow-up on the service provision */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_provision_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_provision_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., caseworker, supervisor, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions for the user (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to access the system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status change event */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual whose status changed */,
  "old_status" TEXT /* Previous status of the individual */,
  "new_status" TEXT /* New status of the individual */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the status change occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the status change */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the status change */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the status change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_changes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_changes_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)
